member.name: P:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.AgentBudget.MaxToolCalls
member.summary: Maximum number of tool invocations allowed across the workflow run.

member.name: P:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.AgentBudget.MaxTokens
member.summary: Maximum total tokens allowed. Tracked via BudgetTracker.RecordTokens — callers (e.g. AgentRuntime) must feed token counts from LLM responses.

member.name: P:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.AgentBudget.MaxExecutionTime
member.summary: Maximum wall-clock time allowed for the entire workflow execution.

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.BudgetTracker
member.summary: Enforces an AgentBudget at runtime. Create one per workflow execution. Thread-safe for concurrent tool calls.

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.BudgetTracker.RecordToolCall
member.summary: Increments the tool-call counter and throws if MaxToolCalls is exceeded. Call this immediately before invoking a tool.

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.BudgetTracker.RecordTokens(System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds to the running total and throws if MaxTokens is exceeded. Feed this from LLM response token counts (e.g. TokenUsage.TotalTokens).

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Execution.Budget.BudgetTracker.CheckTime
member.summary: Throws if MaxExecutionTime has elapsed. Call at the start of each step.

member.name: P:Valaiorp.Execution.Models.TaskNode.ExecutedAt
member.summary: Wall-clock time at which the node began executing. Set by ParallelExecutor.

member.name: P:Valaiorp.Execution.Models.WorkflowStep.ElseStepId
member.summary: Step to jump to when evaluates false. If null and Condition is false, the workflow terminates at this step.

member.name: P:Valaiorp.Execution.Models.WorkflowStep.LoopStartId
member.summary: ID of the matching loop-start step. Required on loop-end steps so the executor can look up the correct iteration counter.

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Execution.Replay.ReplayEngine
member.summary:
Captures per-step state during a workflow run and enables deterministic re-runs from any prior checkpoint (time-travel debugging).
Usage — capture: Pass a ReplayEngine to WorkflowExecutor.ExecuteAsync; snapshots accumulate automatically. Call BuildSnapshot() after execution to persist the result.
Usage — replay: Given a saved ExecutionSnapshot, call RestoreState to seed WorkflowState, then call GetReplaySteps to get the tail of the step list, then re-execute.

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Execution.Replay.ReplayEngine.RestoreState(Valaiorp.Execution.Models.ExecutionSnapshot,System.Int32,Valaiorp.Execution.Models.WorkflowExecutionContext)
member.summary: Restores .WorkflowState to the state that existed immediately after snapshot step - 1 completed. Pass 0 to start a clean replay from the very beginning.

member.name: M:Valaiorp.Execution.Replay.ReplayEngine.GetReplaySteps(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Valaiorp.Execution.Models.WorkflowStep},System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the sub-list of starting at so the executor only re-runs the remaining steps.

member.name: T:Valaiorp.Execution.Transactions.TransactionManager
member.summary:
Stateless transaction coordinator. Each call to RollbackAsync / Commit operates on the ExecutionUnit supplied by the caller, so concurrent BotWorker executions never interfere with each other.
Real rollback: tools that implement have their CompensateAsync called in reverse execution order. Tools that don't implement the interface are marked RolledBack in memory only.
